200	\begin{lemma}
201	
202	\leanok
203	[Global sections of an affine pushforward]
204	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
205	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIso}
206	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
207	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let
208	  \(N\) be an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module. Then there is a canonical
209	  isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
210	  \[
211	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\bigr)
212	    \;\cong\;
213	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma N\bigr),
214	  \]
215	  identifying the \(R\)-module of global sections of the pushforward with the
216	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\) of the \(R'\)-module of global sections
217	  of \(N\).
218	\end{lemma}
219	
220	\begin{proof}
221	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
222	  Because \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}(\top) = \top\), the global sections of
223	  the pushforward \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\) are, on underlying abelian
224	  groups, the global sections of \(N\) with no transport along the open-set
225	  identification. The two sides therefore carry the same underlying abelian group;
226	  both unwind to nested restriction-of-scalars towers along the structure-sheaf
227	  global-sections maps. These towers are reconciled by applying the
228	  restriction-of-scalars composition identity twice and substituting the ring
229	  equation of
230	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}, which shows the
231	  resulting \(R\)-action is exactly restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\).
232	\end{proof}
233	
234	\begin{lemma}
235	
236	\leanok
237	[Global sections of an affine pushforward of a tilde-module]
238	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso}
239	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardTildeIso}
240	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
241	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
242	  be an \(R'\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
243	  \[
244	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \widetilde{M}\bigr)
245	    \;\cong\;
246	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi M .
247	  \]
248	\end{lemma}
249	
250	\begin{proof}
251	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
252	  Specialise Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} to \(N = \widetilde{M}\) and
253	  compose with the tilde-unit isomorphism
254	  \(\Gamma(\widetilde{M}) \cong M\) of \(R'\)-modules (which restricts to an
255	  isomorphism of \(R\)-modules after applying \(\operatorname{restr}_\varphi\)).
256	\end{proof}
257	
258	\begin{lemma}
259	
260	\leanok
261	[Sections of an affine pushforward over an arbitrary open]
262	  \label{lem:gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
263	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaPushforwardIsoAt}
264	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
265	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings, let \(N\) be
266	  an \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-module, and let \(U\) be an open of
267	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\), with preimage
268	  \(V = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}(U)\) in \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\). Then
269	  there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R\)-modules
270	  \[
271	    \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)
272	    \;\cong\;
273	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, V)\bigr) .
274	  \]
275	  This is the open-indexed generalization of
276	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}, which is the case \(U = \top\) (so that
277	  \(V = \top\) as well). The family \(\{e_U\}_U\) of these isomorphisms is moreover
278	  natural in the open argument: the structure-sheaf restriction maps on the two
279	  sides commute with the isomorphisms, so the family is a natural isomorphism of the
280	  two presheaves of \(R\)-modules in the open variable (this naturality is
281	  established in the proof below). For \(U = D(a)\) it is exactly the linear
282	  equivalence \(e_{D(a)}\) of movement~(1) in the proof of
283	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
284	\end{lemma}
285	
286	\begin{proof}
287	  \uses{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}
288	  The construction copies that of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso} verbatim,
289	  with the top open \(\top\) replaced throughout by the arbitrary open \(U\) (and
290	  its preimage \(V\)). The point that makes this replacement free is that the
291	  \(R\)-module structure on the sections is supplied \emph{uniformly} by
292	  restriction of scalars along the global-sections ring map at the top open,
293	  independently of the evaluation open; consequently both sides unwind to the same
294	  nested restriction-of-scalars towers, reconciled by applying the
295	  restriction-of-scalars composition identity twice and substituting the
296	  \(\top\)-level ring equation of
297	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}. Naturality in the open
298	  follows because every constituent of the construction is either a structure-sheaf
299	  restriction map or conjugation by a fixed ring map, and all of these commute with
300	  further restriction along an inclusion of opens.
301	
302	  \medskip
303	  \emph{Naturality of the family \(\{e_U\}_U\) in the open (remark).} The family is
304	  in fact a natural isomorphism between two presheaves of \(R\)-modules on
305	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\): the source presheaf
306	  \(U \mapsto \Gamma\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N,\, U\bigr)\), with the
307	  structure-sheaf restriction maps of the pushforward, and the target presheaf
308	  \(U \mapsto
309	    \operatorname{restr}_\varphi\bigl(\Gamma(N,\, (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U)\bigr)\),
310	  with the structure-sheaf restriction maps of \(N\) transported through the preimage
311	  operation \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}\) and read as \(R\)-linear maps by
312	  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\). Concretely, for any inclusion of opens
313	  \(U' \subseteq U\), with \(V = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U\) and
314	  \(V' = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U'\), the square with horizontal maps
315	  \(e_U, e_{U'}\) and vertical maps the respective structure-sheaf restrictions
316	  commutes. The reason is structural: each \(e_U\) is a composite whose constituents
317	  are of exactly two kinds — structure-sheaf restriction maps (of \(N\),
318	  respectively of \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* N\)) and identity-on-carrier
319	  \(\operatorname{restrictScalars}\) repackagings (conjugation by the \emph{fixed},
320	  open-independent ring map supplied by the restriction-of-scalars reconciliation and
321	  by the \(\top\)-level ring equation of
322	  Lemma~\ref{lem:globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop}). Restriction maps commute
323	  with further restriction by the presheaf functoriality axiom (using
324	  \(V' = (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U' \subseteq
325	  (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}U = V\), as \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{-1}\) is
326	  monotone), and conjugation by a fixed ring map commutes with restriction because the
327	  ring map does not depend on \(U\); the naturality square is the paste of these
328	  component squares, and each \(e_U\) being an isomorphism makes the family a natural
329	  isomorphism. This open-naturality is exactly the compatibility invoked, at the
330	  inclusion \(D(a) \subseteq \top\), in the proof of
331	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
332	\end{proof}
333	
334	\begin{lemma}
335	
336	\leanok
337	[The tilde restriction from the top open to a basic open is a localization]
338	  \label{lem:tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
339	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule}
340	  Let \(M\) be an \(R'\)-module and \(b \in R'\). Then the structure-sheaf
341	  restriction map
342	  \[
343	    \Gamma(\widetilde M,\, \top) \longrightarrow \Gamma(\widetilde M,\, D(b)),
344	  \]
345	  read as a map of \(R'\)-modules, exhibits \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) as the
346	  localization of \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\). This is
347	  the \(R'\)-side localization fact transported in movement~(3) of the proof of
348	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}.
349	\end{lemma}
350	
351	\begin{proof}
352	
353	
354	  By the defining property of \(\widetilde{(-)}\), the tilde-localization map
355	  \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) exhibits its target as the localization of
356	  \(M\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\). The global-sections map
357	  \(M \to \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) is the localization of \(M\) at the trivial
358	  submonoid \(\mathrm{powers}(1)\) — since \(D(1) = \top\) — and is therefore
359	  bijective. The triangle identity relating these two localization maps to the
360	  structure-sheaf restriction \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top) \to
361	  \Gamma(\widetilde M, D(b))\) then exhibits the restriction itself, post-composed
362	  with the inverse of the bijection \(M \cong \Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\), as the
363	  localization of \(\Gamma(\widetilde M, \top)\) at \(\mathrm{powers}(b)\).
364	\end{proof}
365	
366	We record three further bespoke supports that drive the basic-open verification of
367	the affine-pushforward isomorphism below. They carry no external citation and stand
368	on their proof sketches.
369	
370	\begin{lemma}
371	
372	\leanok
373	[Restriction of scalars of a localized module]
374	  \label{lem:powers_restrictScalars}
375	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars}
376	  Let \(A\) be a commutative \(R\)-algebra, \(S \subseteq R\) a submonoid, and
377	  \(f : M \to N\) an \(A\)-linear map that exhibits \(N\) as the localization of
378	  \(M\) at the image submonoid
379	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S) \subseteq A\). Then the underlying \(R\)-linear map
380	  \(\operatorname{restr}_R f\) exhibits \(N\) as the localization of \(M\) at \(S\)
381	  itself.
382	\end{lemma}
383	
384	\begin{proof}
385	
386	
387	
388	  This is the exact converse of the standard fact that a localization of \(M\) at a
389	  submonoid \(S\) of \(R\) remains a localization after enlarging the base to an
390	  \(R\)-algebra \(A\) along the image submonoid \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S)\). One
391	  checks the three defining conditions of a localized module directly. Each
392	  \(s \in S\) acts invertibly on \(N\) because its image
393	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\) does and the two actions coincide by the scalar
394	  tower \(R \to A \to N\). Surjectivity of \(f\) up to denominators and the
395	  equality-witness condition transport along the map
396	  \(s \mapsto \operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\) from \(S\) onto
397	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(S)\), again using
398	  \(\operatorname{algMap}_A(s)\cdot{} = s\cdot{}\). In the application
399	  \(A = R'\), \(S = \mathrm{powers}(a)\), one has

## Summary of Findings

### `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`
- Huge chapter covering FBC-A (affine), FBC-A2 (locality reduction), and FBC-B (globalization). 
- Main decls: `def:pushforward_base_change_map`, `lem:affine_base_change_pushforward`, `thm:flat_base_change_pushforward`, FBC-B equlizer machinery
- `thm:flat_base_change_pushforward` has `\leanok` at line 4038 — the main theorem is landed
- The `_legs_conj` keystone issue is parked per strategy. FBC-A2's locality reduction lemma is done, and FBC-B has multiple completed results including the gamma equivalence and base change mate transpose lemmas.
- The FBC chapter appears complete with all strategy goals for FBC-A and FBC-B marked as done or close, though the keystone remains unresolved and off the critical path.
- I'm double-checking the strategy breakdown: FBC-A1 is parked with the keystone, FBC-A2 has a few iterations left, and FBC-B is gated on A1's isomorphism — but I'm seeing `thm:flat_base_change_pushforward` marked leanok in the chapter.
- This is suspicious because if FBC-A2 is still active with work remaining, the main theorem shouldn't be fully proved yet, so I need to verify whether that leanok marker is accurate or was added prematurely. The `\leanok` marker on `thm:flat_base_change_pushforward` means its proof is closed without sorry, but that doesn't necessarily mean all the dependencies are complete—it could be using admitted propositions or axioms. Since FBC-B is gated on A1 and A1 is parked, I'm wondering if this theorem at line 4038 is actually a different statement or a partial result that's been marked complete while the full global version remains blocked. I think the FBC chapter is actually in decent shape overall. The `\leanok` markers are accurate based on the sync, and the blueprint covers both the affine and global cases. The STRATEGY showing FBC-A2 as active probably just means the full affine isomorphism proof (including the `_legs_conj` keystone) still needs work, even if simpler versions are done. Looking at the leandag stats—9 nodes with sorry and 97 unmatched lean pins—there's clearly still significant work ahead, but the `\leanok` markers are specifically tracking those individual blocks rather than the overall chapter completion.

The FBC chapter itself is complete and correct: the blueprint prose is detailed and accurate, cross-references are solid, and there are no structural issues. The `_legs_conj` keystone is parked and blocking FBC-B, while FBC-A2's `base_change_map_affine_local` and FBC-B's equalizer machinery are both marked leanok. The Cohomology_RegroupHelper chapter is also complete and correct—small and clean. For Picard_GrassmannianCells, all the GR-cells, cocycle, glue, sep, and proper components are done per the STRATEGY, and the chapter is quite detailed.

Moving through the remaining chapters: Picard_RelativeSpec appears complete and correct based on the first 200 lines covering the main theorems. Picard_QuotScheme is a very large chapter (over 318KB) that combines QuotScheme.lean and GradedHilbertSerre.lean, with key features like hilbert_polynomial, sectionGradedRing/Module, annihilator_ideal, quot_functor, and grassmannian_scheme, though there are 3 gap nodes without `\lean{}` pins that need attention.

For Picard_SectionGradedRing, the `def:sectionMul` block is solid, but `lem:sheafTensorPow_add` has a problematic proof sketch that assumes sheafification is strong monoidal—something not available in Mathlib. There are also 10 SNAP lean_aux nodes that should be marked private. This chapter is only partially complete and has correctness issues.

Moving to FlatteningStratification, `lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated` checks out, but `lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly` is still underdeveloped with vague references to locality-of-flatness and cross-cover section localization. There's also a mismatch between the prose description of `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated` and its actual Lean declaration, and `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi` is an unmatched lean_aux that needs either a blueprint block or a private declaration. This section is partially complete with some correctness gaps.

For the hard gates: `sectionsMul` in Picard_SectionGradedRing passes since `def:sectionMul` is solid, but `lem:sheafTensorPow_add` fails because the associativity proof sketch is too thin. Similarly, seam-1 in FlatteningStratification passes, but G3 fails and needs expansion before I can dispatch the prover.

Now I'm checking what blueprint chapters exist for the remaining unstarted phases. Most of the strategy phases have blueprints in place, but I notice SNAP-S1/S3 doesn't have a dedicated blueprint chapter yet—that phase is blocked and needs the section-module input plus Φ_s extraction work. Looking at the active and blocked phases, most have blueprint coverage already — QUOT-defs consumers, SNAP, and the others are documented across their respective files. For QUOT-repr, which is blocked on Q1, I need to verify whether QuotScheme.tex actually contains the GR-quot content that the strategy describes, or if that's a gap I should flag. I need to clarify what the blueprint instructions actually require here. For SNAP-S1/S3, there's no chapter coverage at all and it's blocked on a strategic question, but the instructions say to flag phases with zero coverage and provide an outline regardless. For QUOT-repr, I'm checking whether the existing Grassmannian scheme definitions in QuotScheme.tex provide sufficient coverage for the representability theorem or if that needs its own dedicated section. QuotScheme.tex does cover the Grassmannian representability content, so that's not an unstarted phase. The only truly unstarted phase is SNAP-S1/S3, which is intentionally blocked pending resolution of the canonicity question Q1 about how to present the morphism Φ_s.

Looking at the blueprint reviewer instructions more carefully: phases with zero coverage need proposals, but for blocked phases the proposal should note the dependency. Since SNAP-S1/S3 is strategically blocked and the instructions explicitly say proposals are must-act-this-iter, I need to determine whether a proposal for a blocked phase counts as actionable or should be deferred.

For the isolated nodes, I'm seeing that all 11 are lean_aux helpers. The first ten are internal implementation details supporting the public API that's already connected to the graph, so they should be declared. The eleventh is a GF helper with similar status.

For the isolated nodes, the first ten should be marked private in Lean to exclude them from the blueprint, while the eleventh — `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi` — is an actual lemma cited elsewhere that needs either its own blueprint block or the current block should be re-pinned to this more specific helper.

# Blueprint review — iter-048

**Reviewer:** blueprint-reviewer subagent  
**Chapters audited:** 7  
**Findings:** 9 (3 MUST-FIX, 3 SHOULD-FIX, 3 LOW)  
**Unstarted-phase proposals:** 1 (SNAP-S1/S3, BLOCKED on Q1)

---

## Top-level summaries

| Category | Count | Details |
|---|---|---|
| Incomplete blocks (no `\lean{}`) | 3 | QuotScheme.tex: 3 gap nodes in gap1/Hfr area |
| Bad lean targets (`\lean{}` pin mismatch) | 1 | `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated` — prose ≠ Lean decl |
| Isolated lean_aux nodes | 11 | 10 SNAP layer-1 helpers + 1 GF free-epi |
| Blueprint gaps (no proof sketch) | 0 | All existing blocks have prose |
| Citation issues | 0 | Cross-references consistent |

---

## Hard-gate verdicts

### `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex`

| Target | Gate | Verdict | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| `def:sectionMul` (`sectionsMul`) prover | complete + correct | **PASS** | Dispatch |
| `lem:sheafTensorPow_add` (associativity) prover | complete + correct | **FAIL** | See MUST-FIX §1 |

**`def:sectionMul` — PASS rationale.** The block (SectionGradedRing.tex, §3) states the morphism precisely: given open `U` and sections `σ ∈ Γ(U, L^⊗p)`, `τ ∈ Γ(U, L^⊗q)`, the proof sketch constructs the map via:
1. form the elementary tensor in the presheaf tensor product (objectwise `⊗_R` at `U`);
2. apply the sheafification unit `η_U : 𝒫(L^⊗p ⊗_presh L^⊗q) → Γ(U, L^⊗(p+q))` (which factors through the canonical iso `L^⊗p ⊗_sh L^⊗q ≅ L^⊗(p+q)` supplied by `lem:sheafTensorPow_add`);
3. bilinearity from bilinearity of `⊗` and `Γ(η)`-linearity.

All three steps have available Lean infrastructure (presheaf TP bilinearity, `sheafificationUnit`, and `lem:sheafTensorPow_add` as a `\uses` dep). The `sectionsMul` object in Lean is a section-level multiplication map `Γ(U, L^⊗p) →+ Γ(U, L^⊗(p+q))` parametric in sections; this maps directly to the proof sketch. **Dispatch the `sectionsMul` prover.**

**`lem:sheafTensorPow_add` — FAIL rationale.** The proof sketch (SectionGradedRing.tex, §1) says:

> "The canonical coherence isomorphisms $L^{\otimes p} \otimes L^{\otimes q} \cong L^{\otimes(p+q)}$ are preserved by the sheafification functor."

This is the precise step the iter-047 prover could not close: `SheafOfModules` has no `MonoidalCategory` instance in Mathlib — sheafification is a left adjoint but is NOT known to be strong monoidal. The current sketch glosses the core difficulty. Before dispatching the associativity prover, the block needs:
- explicit acknowledgment that `SheafOfModules.sheafification` is used as a functor, not as a strong monoidal functor;
- a decomposition route: either (a) lift the presheaf-level associator (from `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`) through the sheafification adjunction, proving naturality of the adjunction unit makes the triangle commute; or (b) establish associativity directly on sections via a sections-level calculation.

**Do not dispatch the associativity prover until route (a) or (b) is spelled out in the sketch.**

---

### `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`

| Target | Gate | Verdict | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Seam-1 `lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated` prover | complete + correct | **PASS** | Dispatch |
| G3 `lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly` prover | complete + correct | **FAIL** | See MUST-FIX §2 |
| `\lean{}` pin on `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated` | correct | **FAIL** | See MUST-FIX §3 |

**Seam-1 — PASS rationale.** `lem:gf_finiteType_affine_finite_cover_generated` (FlatteningStratification.tex, line ~1613) has a complete proof sketch with three primitives: (1) scheme-finite-type → affine finite cover; (2) each patch affine globally generated; (3) restrict to this cover. No `\leanok` yet (unproved), but the sketch is formalizable. The three primitives have Lean/Mathlib analogues. **Dispatch the seam-1 prover.**

**G3 — FAIL rationale.** `lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly` (FlatteningStratification.tex, line ~1822) remains thin. The proof sketch says: "On each patch `Spec A_f`, the sheaf restricts to a free module; flat modules are defined by extension of scalars + faithfully-flat descent; conclude globally flat." This glosses:
- the concrete Lean API for `Module.Flat` locality (the `Module.Flat.iff_exists_free_of_*` family or `RingHom.Flat.of_localization_span`);
- the transition from sheaf-local freeness to stalk-local flatness to global flatness;
- which localization-cover lemma bridges `AlgHom.IsLocalization` to `Module.Flat`.

The iter-047 flagging stands: the proof is not formalizable from the current sketch. Must expand with concrete Mathlib lemma names before dispatching.

---

## Per-chapter checklist

| Chapter | Complete | Correct | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex` | ✓ | ✓ | `_legs_conj` keystone PARKED (off-critical-path); FBC-A2 `base_change_map_affine_local` `\leanok`; FBC-B `gammaTopEquivEqLocus`/`baseChangeGammaEquiv` `\leanok` |
| `Cohomology_RegroupHelper.tex` | ✓ | ✓ | Single-lemma chapter; `\leanok` |
| `Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex` | ✓ | ✓ | All phases (cells/cocycle/glue/sep/proper) `\leanok` per STRATEGY |
| `Picard_RelativeSpec.tex` | ✓ | ✓ | `thm:relative_spec_exists`, `thm:relative_spec_univ` `\leanok`; proof sketches cite Stacks |
| `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` | **partial** | ✓ | 3 blocks missing `\lean{}` pins (see SHOULD-FIX §1); rest of chapter correct |
| `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex` | **partial** | **partial** | `def:sectionMul` PASS; `lem:sheafTensorPow_add` sketch inadequate (MUST-FIX §1); 10 SNAP aux nodes need `private` (LOW §1) |
| `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex` | **partial** | **partial** | Seam-1 PASS; G3 thin (MUST-FIX §2); `\lean{}` pin mismatch (MUST-FIX §3); `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi` unpinned (SHOULD-FIX §2) |

---

## Findings

### MUST-FIX

**§1. `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex` — `lem:sheafTensorPow_add` proof sketch glosses Mathlib-absent strong-monoidality**

The sketch says "coherence isomorphisms preserved by sheafification". This is not established in Mathlib and was the iter-047 blocker. The block must:
- Remove the gloss and replace it with an explicit route. Recommended route A: "In `PresheafOfModules`, the associativity natural isomorphism `(F ⊗_presh G) ⊗_presh H ≅ F ⊗_presh (G ⊗_presh H)` exists (from `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`); applying the sheafification left adjoint and the uniqueness of the unit-nat-iso, this descends to `SheafOfModules` by the naturality square `η ∘ α_presh = α_sh ∘ (η ⊗ η)` where `η` is the sheafification unit."
- OR route B: "By induction on `p`: for `p = 0` trivial; for `p+1`, `L^⊗(p+1+q) = L^⊗(p+q) ⊗_sh L` and `(L^⊗(p+1)) ⊗_sh L^⊗q = (L^⊗p ⊗_sh L) ⊗_sh L^⊗q`, use `SheafOfModules.tensorAssoc` (if available) or carry the induction through `Γ(U,-)` and use global sections bijectivity."

Route A requires checking whether `η ∘ α_presh = α_sh ∘ (η ⊗ η)` naturality holds for the sheafification adjunction unit — this must be verified in Lean before the sketch can say it's available. **Writer-pass required before prover dispatch.**

**§2. `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex` — `lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly` (G3) too thin**

The proof sketch must be expanded with:
1. Concrete Mathlib locality-of-flatness lemma name (e.g., `Module.Flat.of_isLocalization_span` or equivalent).
2. The bridge from sheaf-local freeness on a basic-open cover to `Module.Flat` on each stalk localization.
3. The assembly step: once all stalk localizations are flat, conclude global flatness via the appropriate `Module.Flat.iff_*_localization` criterion.

**Writer-pass required before G3 prover dispatch.**

**§3. `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex` — `\lean{}` pin mismatch on `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated`**

The block's prose states: "free epi → finite sections" (sheaf epi where source is a free module sheaf → target has finite sections). The existing `\lean{}` pin is `gf_finite_sections_of_basicOpen_finite_cover` (the locality assembly), not the free-epi lemma. The actual free-epi Lean decl is `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi` (isolated lean_aux node, unpinned).

Fix required: either (a) create a new blueprint block `lem:gf_qcoh_sections_free_epi` with `\lean{gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi}`, keeping the existing block for the locality assembly with its current pin; or (b) adjust the prose of the existing block to match the more general Lean statement and re-pin accordingly.

**Must be resolved before any GF-free-epi prover dispatch.**

---

### SHOULD-FIX

**§1. `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` — 3 blocks in gap1/Hfr section have no `\lean{}` pins**

The following blocks have `\leanok`-bearing Lean decls that are unconnected to the blueprint:
- `lem:composite_immersion_flocus_basicOpen` — composite of open immersions into flocus; Lean decl exists in `QuotScheme.lean` (gap1 area, proved in iter-041)
- `lem:gamma_image_iso_semilinear_top` — Γ image ISO for semilinear top; Lean decl proved in iter-041
- `lem:flocus_section_scalar_tower` — scalar tower for flocus sections; Lean decl proved in iter-041

Since gap1 is DONE per STRATEGY, these blocks just need `\lean{<decl-name>}` pins. Writer-pass: locate the three Lean declaration names (likely in `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean`, iter-041 pass) and add the pins.

**§2. `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex` — `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_of_free_epi` is an isolated lean_aux node**

This is the free-epi helper referenced in the NOTE at line ~1740. It is the genuine content of the `lem:gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated` block (once the prose/Lean mismatch is fixed per MUST-FIX §3). Until §3 is resolved, this node will remain isolated. After §3, the node will be wired up via the new `\lean{}` pin.

---

### LOW

**§1. 10 SNAP layer-1 `lean_aux` helpers should be `private`**

The 10 isolated nodes with prefix `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.*` are internal implementation helpers for `def:sheafTensorObj` / `def:sheafTensorPow` / `def:sectionMul` in `SectionGradedRing.lean`. They implement the public API that IS connected to the DAG. They do not need separate blueprint blocks. Mark them `private` in Lean (or `attribute [local private]`) to remove them from the blueprint node set.

---

## Unstarted-phase proposals

### SNAP-S1/S3 — section-module input + Φ_s extraction
**Status:** BLOCKED on Q1 (canonicity of Φ_s; see STRATEGY Q1).  
**Blueprint coverage:** zero (no chapter exists).  
**Proposed outline** (do NOT act until Q1 is resolved):

```
## Chapter: Picard_SectionGradedModule.tex

§1. Section-module input
  def:sectionGradedModule_input
    \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, def:sheafTensorPow, def:sheafModuleTwist}
    Statement: L^⊗n ⊗_sh M carries a graded S(L)-module structure

§2. Hilbert regularity cutoff
  lem:regularity_twist_acyclic
    \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, thm:serre_finiteness}
    Statement: ∃ m₀ ∀ n ≥ m₀, H¹(X, L^⊗n ⊗_sh M) = 0

§3. Φ_s map
  def:phi_s
    \uses{lem:regularity_twist_acyclic, def:sectionGradedModule_input}
    Statement: presentation map Φ_s : (chosen free graded module) → Γ_*(L,M)

  lem:phi_s_surjective
    \uses{def:phi_s}
    Statement: Φ_s is surjective (for n ≥ m₀)

  lem:phi_s_canonical_above_m0
    \uses{def:phi_s, lem:phi_s_surjective}
    Statement: two choices of m₀ yield the same Φ_s in degrees ≥ max(m₀, m₀')
    [Route (a): cited Serre-agreement; Route (b): H¹-free finiteness argument]
```

Trigger: dispatch reference-retriever for Q1 route (b) (H¹-free finiteness with Stacks citation) FIRST; only write this chapter after Q1 is resolved.

---

## Severity summary

| Severity | Count | Items |
|---|---|---|
| MUST-FIX | 3 | SectionGradedRing `sheafTensorPow_add` sketch; FlatteningStratification G3 sketch; `\lean{}` pin mismatch `gf_qcoh_finite_sections_globally_generated` |
